LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANTS MACREGEN, INC., ROBERT FIELD, LAWRENCE YANNUZZI, CHRISTINE CURCIO, AND B. MICHAEL SILBER'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 128.5 AND 128.7 AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. A "FRIVOLOUS" CLAIM THAT WARRANTS SANCTIONS IS ONE THAT IS "TOTALLY AND COMPLETELY WITHOUT MERIT OR FOR THE SOLE PURPOSE OF HARASSING AN OPPOSING PARTY." (CODE CIV. PROC., 128.5(B)(2).) DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE CLAIMS IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT MEET THAT STANDARD. IT APPEARS THAT THE PARTIES HAVE A GOOD FAITH DISPUTE REGARDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS SUPPOSED TO RECEIVE ACTUAL STOCK CERTIFICATES, WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS APPRISED OF ALL OF THE TERMS IN THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT, AND WHETHER CERTAIN INDIVIDUALS BREACHED THEIR DUTY TO PLAINTIFF, AMONG OTHER THINGS. (SEE MEISTER V. MENSINGER (2014) 230 CAL.APP.4TH 381, 395 ["THERE IS A STRONG PUBLIC INTEREST IN ASSURING THAT CORPORATE OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, MAJORITY SHAREHOLDERS AND OTHERS ARE FAITHFUL TO THEIR FIDUCIARY OBLIGATIONS TO MINORITY SHAREHOLDERS"].) WHILE THE COURT AGREED THAT PLAINTIFF'S UNRUH ACT CLAIM LACKED MERIT, DEFENDANTS DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT SANCTIONS ARE JUSTIFIED. DEFENDANTS' FACTUAL AND LEGAL CHALLENGES TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT MAY BE RAISED BY A FURTHER DEMURRER OR ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANTS IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hostetter, Esq. 408-375-8545 for Plaintiff.
Nathaniel Copper, Esq. 415-693-2195, Patrick Gunn, Esq. 415-693-2070 of Cooley LLP for Defendants Matthew Hemington, Cooley.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MACREGEN, INC., ROBERT FIELD, LAWRENCE YANNUZZI, CHRISTINE CURCIO, B. MICHAEL SILBER's DEMURRER TO KEITH ROIZMAN'S 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant MacRegen, Inc.'s demurrer to the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant causes of action is overruled. The Court takes judicial notice of the Stock Purchase Agreement (SPA). (See Scott v. JPMorgan Chase Bank, NA (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 743, 754.) Although MacRegen contends that the alleged oral employment agreement is inconsistent with the SPA, which contains an integration clause, there is an ambiguity with respect to the relationship between the two agreements. The SPA arguably is an unrelated collateral agreement because 6(k) of the SPA expressly states that "[t]his Agreement is not an employment or other service contract." If it is not an employment contract, then it should not govern the parties' employment relationship. The contract is reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation. (See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ["While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"] As noted in the companion demurrer, however, plaintiff needs to segregate his distinct contractual causes of action against MacRegen and Hemington.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hostetter, Esq. 408-375-8545 for Plaintiff.
Nathaniel Copper, Esq. 415-693-2195, Patrick Gunn, Esq. 415-693-2070 of Cooley LLP for Defendants Matthew Hemington, Cooley.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues causes of action, cites Supreme Court case law. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MATTHEW HEMINGTON, COOLEY LLP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Matthew B. Hemington's demurrer to the third amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and second cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Those claims refer to numerous alleged written and oral contracts, including (1) the June 8, 2015 Common Stock Purchase Agreement; (2) oral employment agreements; (3) oral and written agreements by Hemington to serve as Plaintiff's escrow agent; and (4) an October 12, 2017 written agreement between Defendant MacRegen, Inc. and Plaintiff. (FAC 14-16.) Despite this Court's prior order of June 19, 2019, Plaintiff still has failed to segregate the distinct contractual claims in compliance with Cal. R. Ct. 2.112. Further, as set forth in that order, Plaintiff may state a claim for breach of the SPA only against MacRegen, the only other party to that agreement; the same goes for the alleged oral employment agreements and for the alleged October 12, 2017 agreement with MacRegen. (See, e.g., Seymour v. Christiansen (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 1168, 1179 ["'the individual defendants cannot be liable for breach of the contract or implied covenant since they were not parties to the contract'"]; Clemens v. American Warranty Corp. (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 444, 452 ["Under California law, only a signatory to a contract may be liable for any breach."].) Plaintiff is given one more chance to plead the contract claims separately and against the proper parties.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128 5 And 128 7
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Craig Daniel, Esq. of Gluck Daniel, Tel: 415.510.2509, for defendants and moving party;
Mark Hostetter, Esq. of the Law Offices of Mark Hostetter, Tel: 408.375.8545, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Nathaniel Cooper, Esq. of Cooley LLP, Tel: 415.693.2000, for defendants Cooley LLP etc. on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS MACREGEN, INC., ROBERT FIELD, LAWRENCE YANNUZZI, CHRISTINE CURCIO, AND B. MICHAEL SILBER'S Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP 128.5 And 128.7 as follows:

Defendants' motion for sanctions is denied. A "frivolous" claim that warrants sanctions is one that is "totally and completely without merit or for the sole purpose of harassing an opposing party." (Code Civ. Proc., 128.5(b)(2).) Defendant fails to show that the claims in plaintiff's first amended complaint meet that standard. It appears that the parties have a good faith dispute regarding whether plaintiff was supposed to receive actual stock certificates, whether plaintiff was apprised of all of the terms in the stock purchase agreement, and whether certain individuals breached their duty to plaintiff, among other things. (See Meister v. Mensinger (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 381, 395 ["There is a strong public interest in assuring that corporate officers, directors, majority shareholders and others are faithful to their fiduciary obligations to minority shareholders"].) While the Court agreed that plaintiff's Unruh Act claim lacked merit, defendants do not establish that sanctions are justified. Defendants' factual and legal challenges to plaintiff's claims in the second amended complaint may be raised by a further demurrer or on summary judgment.

Counsel for the defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MACREGEN, INC., ROBERT C. FIELD, LAWRENCE A. YANNUZZI, CHRISTINE A. CURCIO, AND B. MICHAEL SILBER'S DEMURRER TO KEITH ROIZMAN'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT and DEFENDANTS MATTHEW HEMINGTON AND COOLEY LLP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT are heard concurrently.

Appearances:
Mark Hostetter, esq. (408-375-8545) for Plaintiff.
Craig C. Daniel, esq. (415-510-2509) of Gluck Daniel LLP for Defendants Macregen, Inc. Et Al.
Nathaniel r. Cooper, esq. (415-693-2195) of Cooley LLP for Defendants Matthew Hemington, Et Al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MATTHEW HEMINGTON AND COOLEY LLP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend except as noted below. The FAC alleges that Defendant Matthew B. Hemington, while engaging in the conduct alleged in the FAC, acted as Cooley's "agent and partner" and Cooley is therefore liable for his conduct under principles of respondeat superior. (FAC 7.) While a law firm may be vicariously liable for tortious conduct of one of its partners within the scope of his or her employment, Hemington is alleged to have acted only in his capacity as the general counsel, secretary, and chief operating officer of Defendant MacRegen, Inc. (FAC 5.) Further, the SPA recites that Cooley prepared that agreement as counsel to MacRegen, not to Plaintiff or any individual officer or director of MacRegen. (SPA 7(g).) The FAC does not contain any charging allegations against Cooley. Plaintiff has leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, that Hemington was acting in the course and scope of his employment by Cooley in committing any of the alleged actions that could serve as a basis for liability on the part of Cooley.
(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed in open court.
